response:
<<network optimization report>>
kruksal selects edges with lowest weights first to connect all nodes efficiently.
edges chosen include d-e, c-d, b-e, a-d, and e-f for minimum cost.
total cost of this minimum spanning tree sums to exactly fourteen miles.
dijkstra finds shortest path from a to f using original graph weights.
path sequence moves from a to d to e then finally reaches f.
total transportation cost along this specific path equals exactly nine miles.
